_inspectorSnowReport.Report:
3/13 6AM - Good morning and happy Friday, Stratton! For your Fri-Yay, you'll find 60 trails open and groomed top to bottom. Park crew was out all day yesterday working their magic on the features in Betwixt and East Byrnes Side, and they will both reopen, while Tyro will remain closed for today so the crew can get back in there and finish things up right. Alongside, 10 lifts are set to spin at 9AM with potential delays due to high winds. We’re in for a treat, tonight into Saturday, we are looking at 1-3” of new snow, so get excited and keep up those snow dances because winter’s not over yet at Southern Vermont’s highest peak. Have fun, play safe, and I’ll ski ya out there. 

Safety Tip: Never duck a rope; They are there for a reason.

Looking ahead to the weekend, we’re excited for the 14th Annual 24 Hours of Stratton this Saturday. Ski and ride under the lights all night long while enjoying a weekend full of activities, all while raising funds for the Stratton Foundation, which supports Vermont children and families in need. Saturday will also feature the #Shred4Nate alpine slalom race, helping raise awareness for mental health and suicide prevention. Can’t wait to see you there!

Upcoming Events
March 8-14: Women's Week Group Lessons
March 14-15: 24 Hours of Stratton
March 20-22: HOMESICK
March 20: Lunar Laps
March 28: Annual Pond Skim
April 11-12: GATHER
